jokes.text: Maybe if I get lucky...we'll have a little action adventure of our own. You know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Will mentions watching action-adventure movies with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Double entendre suggesting sexual activity using movie genre terminology
jokes.explanation: Solid double entendre that fits Will's character. Not groundbreaking but well-delivered sexual innuendo.

jokes.text: I'm the Man-dingo
jokes.setup: Will asserting his masculinity and control over the evening
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'man' and 'Mandingo' (blaxploitation reference)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's very specific to Will's character and cultural awareness. Economical and memorable.

jokes.text: Lisa will watch what I tell her to watch and she will enjoy it.
jokes.setup: Will's confident assertion of dominance in the relationship
jokes.punchline: Ironic given what we're about to see - that Lisa actually controls the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic sitcom setup for immediate contradiction. The irony pays off immediately with Lisa's entrance.

jokes.text: Terms of Endearment, Fried Green Tomatoes...and The Joy Luck Club. Shall I get us some hankies?
jokes.setup: Audience expects Lisa to go along with Will's action movie choices
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal - she's chosen tearjerker movies instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that completely undercuts Will's earlier bravado. Well-structured reversal that the audience doesn't see coming.

jokes.text: Will's stunned silence/reaction to Lisa's movie choices
jokes.setup: Will's confident assertion that he controls what they watch
jokes.punchline: His speechless reaction to being completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy beat where Will's cocky attitude gets deflated. Visual punchline to his verbal setup.

jokes.text: Have you no shame? Yeah, I'm ashamed of you.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks Will if he has shame about his behavior
jokes.punchline: Will deflects by claiming to be ashamed of Carlton instead
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that perfectly captures their dynamic. Will's deflection is both quick and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Swell. That will go great with your Weezy collectible cups.
jokes.setup: Carlton's earnest excitement about The Jeffersons dinner
jokes.punchline: Will mocking Carlton's dorky collector mentality
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor playing on Carlton's nerdiness and Will's ability to puncture his enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Hey, Kool-Aid!
jokes.setup: Someone (likely Uncle Phil) crashes through something
jokes.punchline: Will's reference to the Kool-Aid Man commercial catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Quick pop culture reference that fits Will's character. Not original but well-timed and economical.

jokes.text: I don't know, Dad, that limbo bar gets as low as 13 inches.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil talking about practicing limbo
jokes.punchline: Carlton's innocent observation about the challenge level
jokes.explanation: Sets up the fat joke perfectly by having Carlton innocently state the facts.

jokes.text: Oh, and sir, the limbo bar is set at three feet when you're ready.
jokes.setup: Previous discussion about limbo and Phil's size
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry delivery about setting the bar higher for Phil
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery that extends the limbo joke while maintaining his character's dry wit.

jokes.text: He couldn't make that lying flat on his back.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey setting the limbo bar at three feet for Phil
jokes.punchline: Will escalating the fat joke to an extreme
jokes.explanation: Escalates the fat jokes but borders on mean-spirited. Economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I want you to meet my father.
jokes.setup: Will asking Lisa to say words that send chills up his spine (expecting something sexy)
jokes.punchline: Lisa wants him to meet her father - the opposite of what he wanted to hear
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts audience expectations. Perfect setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: I got us the Wilt Chamberlain suite. With the electronic scoreboard over the bed and everything.
jokes.setup: Will trying to entice Lisa about their Palm Springs trip
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific sports-themed romantic suite with scoreboard
jokes.explanation: Creative and absurd detail that feels very specific to Will's character. Good visual comedy.

jokes.text: Well, before you and I get into a little one on one...you're gonna have to talk to the head coach.
jokes.setup: Will's basketball metaphors about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Lisa using his own sports metaphor to say he needs her father's approval
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that uses Will's own metaphor against him. Well-constructed wordplay.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, don't be using my analogy against me.
jokes.setup: Lisa using Will's basketball metaphor to make her point
jokes.punchline: Will calling out the comedic structure itself
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor that breaks the fourth wall slightly. Will recognizing his own comedic setup being turned against him.

jokes.text: Oh, not the ears, baby.
jokes.setup: Lisa presumably touching/kissing Will's ears
jokes.punchline: Will's vulnerable reaction to having his ears touched
jokes.explanation: Minor physical comedy moment showing Will's sensitive spots. Not particularly strong but fits the scene.

jokes.text: Keep doing that, we're gonna have to dig up his daddy, I'll talk to him too.
jokes.setup: Will agreeing to talk to Lisa's father
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation suggesting he'd talk to dead grandfathers too if needed
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation showing how far Will would go. Creative but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey, we're not non-profit.
jokes.setup: Customer asking for non-profit book by African-American photographers
jokes.punchline: Will misunderstanding and defending the restaurant's business model
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay misunderstanding that fits Will's character. Quick and effective.

jokes.text: Sorry I'm late...but after I left church, I went down and read some books to the elderly.
jokes.setup: Will trying to impress Lisa's father with good behavior
jokes.punchline: Lisa's almost impossibly wholesome activities
jokes.explanation: Character comedy establishing Lisa as almost too good to be true. Sets up contrast with Will.

jokes.text: Like James Earl Jones. 'This is CNN.'
jokes.setup: Will commenting on Lisa's father's deep voice
jokes.punchline: Impression of James Earl Jones doing CNN tagline
jokes.explanation: Standard impression/reference joke. Not particularly original but fits the moment.

jokes.text: Well, we've only had two food-related deaths in the past month.
jokes.setup: Lisa's father asking about how the food is
jokes.punchline: Will's dark joke about restaurant deaths delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor delivered with perfect deadpan timing. Unexpected and morbid.

jokes.text: It's a joke. I'm joking. I'm pulling your leg.
jokes.setup: Tense moment where Lisa's father seems to be confronting Will aggressively
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was just joking, creating awkward relief
jokes.explanation: Standard tension-breaker moment. Creates discomfort then relief but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: And if you don't mind me saying so, that is a lovely picture of your wife. I see where Lisa gets her good looks from.
jokes.setup: Will trying to compliment Lisa's father by praising his wife's looks
jokes.punchline: Sets up the revelation that it's not his wife
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the upcoming punchline about wrong identification. Well-structured but predictable.

jokes.text: That's not my wife. That's my sister-in-law Cecilia. She's so ugly, she'd make an onion cry.
jokes.setup: Will's compliment about the woman in the photo
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's the ugly sister-in-law with colorful insult
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection payoff with memorable insult. The 'make an onion cry' line is creative and quotable.

jokes.text: Actually, I was being polite. She is a dog.
jokes.setup: Lisa's father insulting his sister-in-law's looks
jokes.punchline: Will agreeing and escalating the insult
jokes.explanation: Continues the ugly joke but with a basic insult. Will trying to bond but using crude humor.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, she's so ugly, she could make a freight train take a dirt road.
jokes.setup: Previous ugly jokes about Cecilia
jokes.punchline: Creative metaphor about her ugliness affecting transportation
jokes.explanation: Creative and memorable ugly joke with good visual imagery. More clever than typical insult humor.

jokes.text: Train whistle and sound effects implying the train changing course
jokes.setup: The freight train taking a dirt road joke
jokes.punchline: Physical demonstration with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Physical extension of the verbal joke. Adds visual comedy but not exceptionally creative.

jokes.text: You talking about my family, boy?
jokes.setup: Will and father bonding over ugly jokes about Cecilia
jokes.punchline: Sudden aggressive turn suggesting Will has gone too far
jokes.explanation: Good tension moment that creates uncertainty about the father's real feelings. Effective misdirection.

jokes.text: You know what, I like you.
jokes.setup: Tense confrontational moment where father seems angry
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal - he actually likes Will
jokes.explanation: Classic tension/relief structure. Not original but well-executed with good timing.

jokes.text: 'Meticulous.' Hey, now that's a word you don't run into every day.
jokes.setup: Will using the word 'meticulous' in conversation
jokes.punchline: Father commenting on the vocabulary level
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing the father's appreciation for education/vocabulary. Mild but fits the scene.

jokes.text: Say, why don't you fly to Frisco with me? You can help me with my vocabulary.
jokes.setup: Father impressed with Will's vocabulary
jokes.punchline: Invitation that will lead to disaster (ironic given what's coming)
jokes.explanation: Sets up the main plot. The irony becomes apparent as their flight goes horribly wrong.

jokes.text: Me, scared? Hell, no.
jokes.setup: Father asking if Will is scared to fly
jokes.punchline: Will's bravado that will be immediately contradicted
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate contradiction. Will's false bravado before plane terror.

jokes.text: Hey, do you think you could just land the plane, you know...and I'll just walk back to L.A.
jokes.setup: Will's terror about flying
jokes.punchline: Absurd request to walk back from San Francisco
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist fear reaction. The geography makes it funnier - walking from SF to LA.

jokes.text: Oh, flying upside down makes you a little queasy, doesn't it, boy?
jokes.setup: Will's obvious terror and motion sickness
jokes.punchline: Father's understated observation about upside-down flying
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery understating the extreme situation. Father's casual attitude to dangerous flying.

jokes.text: You might as well admit it, boy, you're like a squirrel trying to get a nut.
jokes.setup: Conversation about Will's intentions with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Colorful metaphor with sexual double entendre
jokes.explanation: Creative sexual metaphor that fits the father's direct speaking style. Good imagery and wordplay.

jokes.text: How many times did you put those rusty, crusty lips on my daughter?
jokes.setup: Father interrogating Will about his relationship with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Graphic and insulting way to ask about kissing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the intimidating father character. Crude but effectively uncomfortable.

jokes.text: The engine's dead.
jokes.setup: Father's aggressive interrogation of Will
jokes.punchline: Casual announcement of life-threatening mechanical failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic escalation. Moves from relationship drama to life-threatening crisis with casual delivery.

jokes.text: Faster than a bucket of chicken at Oprah's.
jokes.setup: Will asking if they're going down fast
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference about Oprah's supposed eating habits
jokes.explanation: Memorable and specific pop culture reference. May be dated but creative and economical.

jokes.text: Let me tell you, I'm from Philly...you've gotta wake up pretty early in the morning to get me with some old...
jokes.setup: Will building up his street credibility and toughness
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off mid-sentence (presumably by crash or fear)
jokes.explanation: Classic Will bravado getting interrupted by reality. Good character moment showing his false confidence.

jokes.text: Your damn chute wasn't supposed to open.
jokes.setup: Will celebrating his survival
jokes.punchline: Father implying he sabotaged the parachute
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor revealing the father's extreme protective measures. Shocking and memorable.

jokes.text: Your plane wasn't supposed to crash neither. I guess you just having an all-around bad day, huh?
jokes.setup: Father's revelation about sabotaging the parachute
jokes.punchline: Will's understated response to the attempted murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to an extreme situation. Will's ability to stay cool under pressure.

jokes.text: Our precise position is somewhere...over some trees and rocks and stuff.
jokes.setup: Radio mayday call requiring precise location
jokes.punchline: Will's completely unhelpful description of wilderness
jokes.explanation: Good city boy in wilderness humor. Will's complete inability to read natural landmarks.

jokes.text: Mominimum? Don't you understand English, boy? Mominimum.
jokes.setup: Father using made-up word 'mominimum' for momentum
jokes.punchline: Will's confusion and father's insistence on the wrong word
jokes.explanation: Creative malapropism showing father's personality. Funny made-up word that sounds plausible.

jokes.text: Good news. We found the plane. It's totaled. That's good news?
jokes.setup: Ranger announcing good news about the search
jokes.punchline: The 'good news' is that the plane is destroyed
jokes.explanation: Good ironic misunderstanding of what constitutes good news. Phil's natural reaction.

jokes.text: If I knew that, I'd be working for the Psychic Friends Network.
jokes.setup: Phil asking where Will is
jokes.punchline: Ranger's sarcastic reference to 90s psychic hotline
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference but fits the character's sarcastic attitude. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: So you're gonna be twice as upset if they're dead, huh?
jokes.setup: Lisa saying that's her boyfriend and father out there
jokes.punchline: Ranger's insensitive mathematical observation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing the ranger's insensitive but logical thinking. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Now, you listen here, Mr. Park-Ranger- Psychic-Friends-Network-freak.
jokes.setup: Ranger's unhelpful and insensitive comments
jokes.punchline: Ashley's angry string of insults incorporating earlier joke
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback. Ashley's rare moment of aggression makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Look, a party sounds like a lot of fun, but can we please find Will first?
jokes.setup: Ranger mentioning sending out a search party
jokes.punchline: Carlton's literal interpretation of 'party'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton misunderstanding showing his literal-minded nature. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: This is our stuff!
jokes.setup: Will excited about finding 'civilization' (backpacks and radio)
jokes.punchline: Realizes they've been walking in circles, finding their own gear
jokes.explanation: Classic survival comedy trope - lost people finding their own trail. Well-executed recognition moment.

jokes.text: We wasted four hours traveling around this stupid mountain.
jokes.setup: Realization they've been walking in circles
jokes.punchline: The full scope of their incompetence revealed
jokes.explanation: Extends the circular walking joke but doesn't add much. Functional but not creative.

jokes.text: Stop spouting all that witchcraft.
jokes.setup: Will trying to use basic wilderness survival knowledge
jokes.punchline: Father dismissing navigation skills as witchcraft
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing father's suspicious nature. Funny contrast between knowledge and superstition.

jokes.text: What the hell does that mean?
jokes.setup: Father's confusing metaphor about 'double order of lip' and 'short stack of patience'
jokes.punchline: Will's confusion at the mixed-up restaurant metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to confusing metaphor. Will expressing what audience is thinking.

jokes.text: Please, you're like 479 years old, right?
jokes.setup: Father wanting to fight Will
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated estimate of father's age
jokes.explanation: Standard age exaggeration joke. Fits Will's character but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Oh, fine, that makes it easier for me. Old man, huh? I've got you now.
jokes.setup: Will calling him old and refusing to fight
jokes.punchline: Father using the insult as motivation to attack
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation to physical comedy. Sets up the fight sequence but not particularly witty.

jokes.text: Coyote howling sounds
jokes.setup: Will and father fighting in the wilderness
jokes.punchline: Threatening animal sounds adding to their predicament
jokes.explanation: Standard survival comedy escalation. Adds tension but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Right now I bet George is calling somebody a jive turkey.
jokes.setup: Carlton missing The Jeffersons live show
jokes.punchline: His specific knowledge of George Jefferson's catchphrases
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton obsession with classic TV. Shows his detailed knowledge of the show.

jokes.text: Oh, don't worry. If he can survive there...a couple of snakes and bears aren't gonna bother him.
jokes.setup: Phil worried about Will surviving in the woods as a city boy from West Philly
jokes.punchline: Ranger's observation that West Philly is more dangerous than wilderness
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about urban vs. natural dangers. Fits both the setting and Will's background.

jokes.text: And that was just the mild.
jokes.setup: Explosive reaction to hot sauce fire attempt
jokes.punchline: Revealing it wasn't even the hottest sauce
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke. If mild sauce did that, imagine the hot. Economical punchline.

jokes.text: That ain't happening with us.
jokes.setup: Will's heartfelt speech about men bonding through adversity
jokes.punchline: Father's flat rejection of any bonding possibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of Will's romantic wilderness bonding speech. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Not even a little.
jokes.setup: Already rejecting bonding with Will
jokes.punchline: Doubling down on the rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to previous rejection. Maximally economical and devastating to Will's hopes.

jokes.text: The defense rests.
jokes.setup: Will defending modern hip-hop with examples like Guru and A Tribe Called Quest
jokes.punchline: Father's legal metaphor dismissing the entire genre
jokes.explanation: Clever legal metaphor showing the father's dismissive attitude. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: But don't you care what she wants? - Nope.
jokes.setup: Will's reasonable question about Lisa's autonomy
jokes.punchline: Father's immediate, blunt rejection of the concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing father's old-school protective attitude. Economical and honest.

jokes.text: I mean, that's so much responsibility. I really should be out there looking for your cousin.
jokes.setup: Ranger talking about preventing forest fires responsibility
jokes.punchline: Suddenly remembering he should actually be doing his job
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing the ranger's scattered priorities. Ironic awareness of his actual duties.

jokes.text: Don't worry. Will likes to sleep in.
jokes.setup: Ranger saying he should look for Will
jokes.punchline: Carlton's casual dismissal of the urgency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton obliviousness to the serious situation. Treats plane crash like oversleeping.

jokes.text: Kind of looked like the beer commercials. Except there wasn't no naked honeys. There wasn't no beer.
jokes.setup: Will describing the beautiful wilderness setting
jokes.punchline: Noting what was missing from the beer commercial fantasy
jokes.explanation: Good Will-specific observation comparing reality to fantasy. Shows his priorities and pop culture awareness.

jokes.text: But he said, 'No, you go on without me...I wanna stay here and be at one with nature.'
jokes.setup: Will explaining why Lisa's father didn't return
jokes.punchline: Fabricating a spiritual awakening story
jokes.explanation: Good ironic lie covering for abandoning Lisa's father. Will's self-serving storytelling.

jokes.text: You can't be at one when you two.
jokes.setup: Will's claim that father wants to be 'at one with nature'
jokes.punchline: Carlton's literal mathematical interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton literal-mindedness. Treats spiritual metaphor as math problem. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Outside is a trail of hot sauce. Light a match, take you right to him.
jokes.setup: Father stranded and looking for Will
jokes.punchline: Reference to hot sauce explosion creating a trail
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier physical comedy. Creates visual of hot sauce trail through wilderness.

jokes.text: Red Rooster? Anybody, give me a hand. Squirrel! I need... Give me a piece of cheese, I'm hungry.
jokes.setup: Father desperately calling for help in the wilderness
jokes.punchline: Progression from people to fairy tale characters to asking animals for food
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing father's desperation and descent into fantasy. Creative progression of appeals for help.
